codeReview.summary: The fix closes the original obvious gap for successful and typed-failure effects and adds direct durationMs assertions, but withSpan still does not record durationMs for all span end paths. Because it measures time around Effect.either(effect), defects and interruptions bypass the annotateCurrentSpan call, so some ended spans still lack the required durationMs attribute.

issues.description: withSpan measures duration around Effect.either(effect), which only converts typed failures. If the wrapped effect dies or is interrupted, execution never reaches the durationMs annotation at line 41, so spans that end through those paths still miss the required durationMs attribute. I reproduced this against the built package with Effect.die("boom"): the collected span ended with error metadata but no durationMs attribute.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance is still inconsistent about telemetry span attributes: the validation contract for VAL-INFRA-012 only lists host, operation, and exit code, while both the feature metadata and the project architecture notes require durationMs too. Clarifying the contract would make future implementations and reviews less error-prone.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md:61-62 omits durationMs; features.json:887 and 948-955 require durationMs; .factory/library/architecture.md:42-43 lists durationMs as a standard telemetry attribute.

summary: Fail. The fix adds durationMs for the common success and typed-failure cases and improves test coverage, but withSpan still skips durationMs when an effect dies or is interrupted, so the helper does not yet record durationMs on every span end path.
